description: Scoped, security-leaning Renovate config for the mcm monorepo (drafted 2026-07-05).
Every Renovate PR runs the normal guardrails + app-ci gates; cd-deploy only fires on merge
to main, so nothing auto-deploys. Nothing is auto-merged — review + let CI gate.
The high-value slice is base-image + Actions + Cargo + Python patching (pairs with the Trivy
gate in cd-deploy). React Native / Expo is deliberately NOT bumped by Renovate (see below).
Open PRs for known-vulnerable deps promptly (the actual security value; complements the SAST/Trivy gates).
osvVulnerabilityAlerts (above) is REQUIRED here: this runs on Forgejo, which has no GitHub-style
advisory API, so native vulnerabilityAlerts alone would be inert — OSV is the working source and
matches what `pnpm audit` / the SAST gate flag.
minimumReleaseAge is dropped to 1 day (vs the 3-day global supply-chain cooldown): a targeted
security patch from an existing dep should remediate FAST — the churn this codifies against was CVEs
sitting un-fixed for days while every PR's SAST/Trivy gate went red (fast-uri/brace-expansion/js-yaml/
shell-quote/axios/tar, 2026-07). 1 day still clears the initial compromised-release window (feature 034).

packageRules.description: Supply-chain cooldown (feature 034): hold every update until the release is ≥3 days old, mitigating the compromised-just-published-release window. Applies globally via a catch-all match.

Never bump our OWN registry images — cd-deploy owns those digests (digest-by-git promotion). Matched by the jumbleknot namespace, NOT the forge host literal (topology-scrub gate forbids the host in git).

React Native / Expo are version-locked: Expo SDK + RN upgrades MUST go through `expo install` / the expo upgrade skill, not raw Renovate bumps, or the mobile build breaks in subtle ways. Disable the RN/Expo core in the app package.

React is version-locked to the Expo SDK across the WHOLE workspace, not just the app. react/react-dom also live in the root package.json and packages/design-system — the previous app-only ignore let Renovate bump those two, splitting React (root/design-system ahead of the Expo-pinned app). A split React = two physical copies = invalid-hook-call at runtime. Disable react/react-dom/react-test-renderer repo-wide so they only ever move together via the expo upgrade skill.
Group Docker base-image updates into one PR per image (postgres, keycloak, mongo, opensearch, vault, node, caddy, langfuse, clickhouse…). This is the main security win.
Non-app JS/TS deps (scripts, mcm-app NON-RN deps, workspace tooling): majors separate + never auto-merged.

Non-app JS/TS patch + minor updates grouped into ONE PR to cut churn — the majors rule above long CLAIMED this but no rule implemented it, so each npm patch/minor opened its own PR. Majors stay separate (rule above); the RN/Expo/react disables above still win (they set enabled:false and this rule doesn't override that).
